title: Site Process Discovery Playbook
description: Run a site process discovery before blaming hardware or support: observe, earn trust, match bench results to local process, present root cause to decision makers.

mindset.principle: Data without floor context loses. Floor without data is anecdote. Do both, in that order when politics are hot.

mistakes.label: Present NDF charts before watching their process
mistakes.cost: They reject the data; you miss the real cause

mistakes.label: Treat every field failure as parts failure
mistakes.cost: Replace loops, vendor fights, customer still broken

mistakes.label: Publicly embarrass local techs without a fix path
mistakes.cost: You win the room and lose the account

common_false_hw_patterns.label: Mass software push reboots fleets; OS corruption looks like disk fail
Power strip or breaker used as shutdown; write damage / boot failure
Identical units on bench; wrong unit returned
Power quality, heat, dust misread as intermittent HW

decision_branches.situation: High-visibility fail claims; blame on vendor or support
Returns test good; field still replaces
Need leadership decision on process change
Local IT cooperative but central IT hostile process

steps.name: Prep: pull account NDF and top SKUs
Brief decision makers with fix path
steps.goal: Change process, not just parts policy

agent_signal.use_when: Account blames hardware at scale. Returns often NDF. Need site visit pattern: observe process before exec data meeting.
decision_criteria: Classify root cause: HW vs process vs OS/env.
Present fix path, not only blame.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: many institutional false HW storms are local process (updates, power, swaps) plus good bench data.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic job spine comms. Not a substitute for mf-tpl-060 return log basics. Not legal advice.
trigger_phrases: Account blames hardware at scale. Returns often NDF. Need site visit pattern: ob
Need playbook: Site Process Discovery Playbook
